90-2007

[Fresno County Seal]

Doug Vagim
Supervisor, District 3

Board of Supervisors

Mar 19 7:56 AM '90

March 15, 1990

CALIFORNIA F.P.P.C.
Legal Division
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Subject: Expansion of Opinion in File No. I-89-688

In the previous opinion you ruled that I am a subcontractor for
a project that involves the Fresno Metropolitan Flood Control
District (F.M.F.C.D.). I currently participate in the project
as the system analyst. I'm also named as a member of the
project team as per the contract (copy previously sent as per
option I-89-688).

In the last twelve months, the project has been a source of
income to me greater than $250.00. In opinion I-89-688
(footnote 6) you advised that because of public agency
exemption a conflict does not exist between me as a member of
the board of supervisors and my role as a subcontractor with
F.M.F.C.D. However, I feel the finding needs more examination.

ISSUE

The County Board of Supervisors has discretionary powers over
the annual distribution of the special district property tax
augmentation funds. F.M.F.C.D. is one of the agencies within
Fresno County contending for these funds. In addition when
F.M.F.C.D. seeks to add land to its district and receive a
percentage from the property tax base, it must receive approval
from the Fresno County Board of Supervisors.

QUESTION

1. May I participate in the decision making process for
the distribution of the property tax augmentation funds
to:

A. F.M.F.C.D. as a recipient of the funds?

B. All other districts that are contending for the
same funds?

Room 300, Hall of Records/2281 Tulare Street/Fresno, California 93721-2198/(209) 488-3531/1-800-742-1011
Equal Employment Opportunity — Affirmative Action — Handicap Employer

F.P.P.C. Possible Conflict of Interest
March 15, 1990
Page 2


QUESTION CONTINUED

2.  May I participate in decision making process when
F.M.F.C.D.:

A.  Is seeking to expand its district size in the
unincorporated area of the County?

B.  Requires part of Fresno County's property tax base
on the land included in the Districts expansion to
be transferred to F.M.F.C.D.?

Thank you very much for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

Doug Vagim

DOUG VAGIM
Fresno County Supervisor

:cwr

Doug Vagim
Supervisor, District 3
Board of Supervisors

April 6, 1990

Mr. John Wallace, Esq.
California P.P.P.C.
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-807

Re: Letter No. 90-207

Dear Mr. Wallace:

I am forwarding to you, as per our conversation, the agenda item
that dealt with M.F.C.D. asking for a 2.2% increase in its
drainage fees. Please add this question in with the others I
previously sent to you on this matter.

In addition, for informational purposes, I am including a finding
by the Fresno County Grand Jury questioning my participation with
the special district augmentation fund and M.F.C.D.

Thank you for your help.

Sincerely,

Doug Vagim

Room 300, Hall of Records/2281 Tulare Street/Fresno, California 93721-2198/(209) 488-3531/1-800-742-1011
Equal Employment Opportunity – Affirmative Action – Handicap Employer

County of
FRESNO
APR 10 8 25 AM '90

Agenda Item

DATE: March 27, 1990

TO: Board of Supervisors

FROM: Richard D. Welton, Director [illegible signature]
Public Works and Development Services Department

SUBJECT: Adoption of Resolution Establishing a New Schedule of Drainage
Costs and Fees pursuant to Section 17.64.050 of the County
Ordinance Code

RECOMMENDED ACTION:

Adopt a resolution establishing the per-acre costs and fees related to the
Storm Drainage and Flood Control Master Plan.

FISCAL IMPACT:

No fiscal impact to the County. The schedule of costs and fees for the
funding of planned drainage facilities is required by Ordinance to be reviewed
by your board annually. The revised schedule of costs and per gross acre fees
is calculated to raise the sum of money necessary to pay the estimated cost of
local drainage facilities in each local drainage area.

DISCUSSION:

In 1961, the Cities of Fresno and Clovis and the County of Fresno adopted as
an element of the Fresno-Clovis Metropolitan Area General Plan, a storm
drainage master plan prepared by the Flood Control District for the
metropolitan area. In 1969, these agencies adopted virtually identical
drainage fee ordinances to provide for the funding of planned drainage
facilities concurrently with development activity which occasioned the need
for such facilities.

The local drainage plan shows the drainage area boundaries. Also shown are
proposed and existing master plan facilities in each watershed area. An area
currently receiving permanent drainage service from existing master plan
facilities is crosshatched on the plan.

ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE REVIEW [illegible signature] Page 2 of 2
BOARD ACTION: DATE _______ APPROVED AS RECOMMENDED _____ OTHER _____

UNANIMOUS _____ ANDREEN _____ CONRAD _____ KOLIGIAN _____ LEVY _____ VAGIM _____
FC-17 (1989 REV)

31

31

The rate structure associated with each master plan drainage area is based on
the estimated total cost of the urban drainage system including land
improvements and engineering. The costs of proposed facilities are estimated
divided into the total land area using appropriate proportionate ratios
between varying land uses.

It is important to note that drainage fees paid pursuant to the Fresno
Metropolitan Flood Control District administered ordinance are deposited into
a separate trust account. The monies are not commingled with District general
funds, are not used to fund any administration, operations or maintenance
costs, and must be expended within the drainage area in which they are paid.

The fee ordinance, enacted under the authority of the State Subdivision Map
Act, requires the identification of the estimated or actual cost of the
planned facilities through the adoption of a resolution. Because the fees are
based on the cost of facilities, the resolution serves also as the schedule of
fees.

The Ordinance permits annual adjustments in the schedule of costs and fees
based upon changes in the National Engineering News Record Construction Cost
Index or based upon locally experienced changes in system costs. The
adjustment is to be made March 1st of each year.

Fresno Metropolitan Flood Control District has recommended that the ENR Index
update be used this year. They state that changes in the Master Plan are
minimal relative to the need to recalculate system costs for the I3D drainage
systems. The adjustment is uniform 2.2% increase pursuant to the index.

OTHER REVIEWING AGENCIES:

The 2.2% increase of ENR Index has been reviewed and approved by the BIA.

3511Q

FRESNO COUNTY GRAND JURY

ROOM 103, COURTHOUSE
FRESNO, CALIFORNIA 93721

February 16, 1990

The Honorable Mario Olmos
Presiding Judge of the Superior Court
County of Fresno, Department 6
1100 Van Ness
Fresno, California 93721

A. Vernon Conrad, 1989 Chairman
Fresno County Board of Supervisors
2281 Tulare Street
Fresno, California 93721

Dear Judge Olmos and Chairman Conrad:

The 1989-90 Fresno County Grand Jury (GJ Jury) is
issuing this Final Report #2, in answer to Superior Conrad's
request to investigate allegations between a
Supervisor's Member and the County Administrative Officer. The
Grand Jury has focused its investigation on County policy and
procedure.

Respectfully Submitted,

Melba Jeffress, Foreman

MJ/sa
Enclosure

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or very faintly printed, with only minimal visible text. I can make out what appears to be a page number or marking near the bottom right that looks like it might be "K" or a similar character, and there is some very faint text along the bottom margin that is too illegible to read with confidence.

Since the document is essentially blank or the text is too faint to read reliably, I cannot provide an accurate transcription without risking inaccuracy.

Finding No. 2. On September 26, 1989, the Board discussed
Agenda Item #8, "Public Hearing on Distribution of Special
District Augmentation Funds for 1989-90." This item dealt with
the allocation of funds to member district, which includes the
Fresno Metropolitan Flood Control District. The staff
recommended a district allocation of $475,000 rather than the
district request of $750,000; this was a decrease of $75,000
from the previous year's allotment of $550,000. In preparing
its recommendations to the Board, County staff used the general
guidelines set by the Board in previous years.

The Grand Jury understands from Vagim that, while he has a
consulting relationship with the Metropolitan Flood Control
district, he does not have a direct contract with them, but
rather receives compensation for his services through his
brother's firm. This arrangement has raised the question of a
possible conflict of interest.

-3-

SERVICE AGREEMENT
CALCULATION OF BENEFIT ASSESSMENTS
REDBANK-PANCHER CREEKS FLOOD CONTROL PROJECT

This Agreement is made and entered into this /6 day of
February, 1987, between the FRESNO METROPOLITAN FLOOD CONTROL DIS-
TRICT, a California public agency, hereinafter referred to as
"District" and ALPHAMERIC DATA PROCESSING, a California corpora-
tion, hereinafter referred to as "Contractor".

WHEREAS, the District is the "Local Project Sponsor" of
the Redbank-Pancher Creeks Flood Control Project, hereinafter
referred to as the "Project"; and

WHEREAS, the District, as the Local Project Sponsor, is
responsible for providing, as the local share, all Project lands,
easements, rights-of-way and local share cash contributions to the
construction of the Project's features; and

WHEREAS, it is anticipated that the total local share of
the Project cost shall be secured through the levying of the Dis-
trict's authorized property assessment, which assessment shall be
placed on the annual Fresno County tax bill of all lands receiving
flood control, water resource or indirect economic benefits from
said Project pursuant to the Capital and Operating Revenue Assess-
ment Levy attached hereto as Exhibit "A" and hereinafter referred
to as the "Assessment Levy"; and

WHEREAS, the Board of Directors of the District has
requested written proposals from all interested computer service
bureaus for computerized systems development, programming and
operation to effect the calculation, levy and collection of the
Assessment Levy; and

WHEREAS, the Contractor represents and warrants that it
has the qualifications to provide the required computer services;
and

Item II

WHEREAS, the Contractor has submitted a bid to the Dis-

trict to provide such computer services to the District; and

WHEREAS, the Contractor and the District desire that

Contractor shall provide such computer services to the District on

the terms hereinafter set forth and that they be bound by this

Agreement; and

WHEREAS, the District and Contractor agree that the

following provisions shall apply to and shall govern the perfor-

mance of the work and services to be provided by Contractor under

this Agreement.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of their mutual cove-

nants and conditions, the parties hereto agree as follows:

1. TERM. The term hereof shall be from the date hereof

until January 31, 1990, unless either extended or sooner termi-

nated pursuant to the terms hereof. District may extend this

Agreement for successive one-year terms by giving Contractor writ-

ten notice at least ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of

the then-current term hereof.

2. DUTIES OF THE CONTRACTOR.

A.(1) Contractor shall, in accordance with the

highest industry standards, provide all work, services, mate-

rials, and resources required to perform its duties as set

forth hereinbelow. Such work, services, materials and re-

sources shall include, but not be limited to, labor, equip-

ment, programming, computer system capacity and capability,

data, data management and manipulation, forms, reports, inter-

faces, system access, consulting, training and design.

(2) Contractor shall perform that analysis, system

design and programming necessary to levy assessments as

required by this contract. This work shall be hereinafter

referred to as "Preliminary Services" and shall be completed

2

in such manner and at such time as will allow contractor to
timely meet all of its other obligations hereunder.

(3) All work, services, materials and resources
shall be the property of the District and shall not be sub-
ject to any liens in favor of Contractor or any third party.
Contractor's programs shall employ the COBOL ANSI language.
As Contractor prepares each program or file called for in the
subparagraphs hereinbelow, it shall forthwith deliver a copy
thereof in magnetic tape form and hard copy to the District
along with all flow charts, programs, descriptions, specifi-
cations, system documentation, program source codes, object
codes, operator procedure instruction manuals, user procedure
manuals, data; file definitions and related information and
shall confer with District's designated representative to
fully explain that which is being delivered. In addition,
Contractor shall maintain back-up copies of all such magnetic
tapes in proper off-site storage facilities, such tapes to be
available to District at all times upon demand.

B. Prior to April 1 of each year during the term
of this Agreement, Contractor shall acquire from the County
of Fresno in magnetic tape form the then-current March 1 lien
data roll, the NAV (Names, Addresses, Values) File and the
AIS (Appraisal Information System) File (the relevant data
portions only) for all parcels located within the Zones of
Benefit identified by District pursuant to Exhibit "A" hereto,
as such Zones may from time-to-time be revised.

C.(1) No later than April 1 of each year during the
term of this Agreement, Contractor shall prepare and deliver
to District in magnetic tape form and hard copy a Master File
of all parcels within each zone of benefit, which Master File
shall be based upon the most recent March 1 lien date roll

3

and the then-current NAV and AIS property files of the County
of Fresno. Such Master File shall contain the following
property information; assessor's parcel number, zone of bene-
fit, land use, parcel size, owner's name and address, assess-
ment factor, assessment rate and assessment amount.

(2) Prior to April 10 of each year during the term
of this Agreement, Contractor shall acquire from the County
of Fresno the property information changes which occurred
prior to March 1 of said year, but which were not yet posted
to the County March 1 lien date roll and the related NAV and
AIS Files. All such changes shall be used by Contractor to
update the District's Master File no later than May 15 of
each year.

D. No later than April 10 of each year during the
term of this Agreement, Contractor shall submit to District a
list of any and all parcels included in the Master File for
which the County's March 1 lien date roll, NAV and AIS Files
contain no acreage and/or land use data or invalid land use
data. District shall provide such items of data for said
parcels to the Contractor as soon as practicable and in no
event shall District provide such items of data later than
May 1 of each such year. Contractor shall enter such data
onto the Master File no later than May 15 of each year during
the term of this Agreement and provide a copy thereof to
District along with the supporting information described in
Subparagraph A hereinabove. To the extent that after April 23
of each year during the term of this Agreement District pro-
vides Contractor items of data in regard to more than four
thousand (4,000) assessor's parcel numbers, said date of
May 15 shall be extended by one (1) day for each eight hun-
dred (800) parcels (or any portion thereof) in excess of such

4

four thousand (4,000) parcels. Contractor shall maintain
such changes to data received from the County pursuant to
Subparagraph 2.B. hereof and the land use and area data
received from the District pursuant to Subparagraph 2.B.
hereof in the Supplemental File in the form and for the time
period necessary to achieve the purposes of this Agreement.

E. Prior to June 15 of each year during the term
of this Agreement, Contractor shall, using the Master File
and the assessment factors and rates supplied by the District
pursuant to Subparagraph 4.F. of this Agreement, compute the
specific per parcel assessment for each parcel within each
zone of benefit, shall formulate the Preliminary Annual
Assessment Roll and provide to District a copy, along with
the supporting information therefor described in Subparagraph
A. hereinabove. Not later than July 2, District shall submit
such preliminary roll to the County of Fresno for a test run
by the County in the form shown on Exhibit "B" hereto. The
assessment calculated for each parcel shall be divisible by
two. The Contractor may be required to provide additional
test runs for District as necessary to ensure accuracy of the
Final Annual Assessment Roll or to amend, monitor, or analyze
(i) total assessment revenues, (ii) revenue by land use or
zone of benefit or (iii) other aspects of the assessment
program.

F. Prior to July 7 of each year during the term of
this Agreement, following the June 30 certification by the
County of the final tax roll, Contractor shall obtain from
the County the final list of revisions concerning parcel
splits and changes of ownership for all parcels within the
Zones of Benefit and shall obtain from the District correc-
tions it may have to the Preliminary Annual Assessment Roll

5

previously submitted to the District. Contractor shall,

prior to July 15 of each year during the term of this Agreement, use such data to update District's Master File, Supplemental File and Preliminary Annual Assessment Roll and shall

produce a Final Annual Assessment Roll from such update and

provide a copy thereof to District, along with that supporting information described in Subparagraph A hereinabove.

G. Contractor shall prepare and submit the Final

Annual Assessment Roll to the County no later than July 15 of

each year during the term of this Agreement. The Final Annual

Assessment Roll prepared by the Contractor shall be a magnetic

computer tape which shall possess the following characteristics and file sequence:

(1) Characteristics:     No header or trailer labels,
                         Nine-track 1600 BPI
                         Eighty bytes per record
                         Unblocked
                         EBCDIC

(2) File Sequence:       Position      Data Element Name
                         From To
                         1    1        Transaction Code
                         2    10       Assessor's Parcel
                         11   31       Filler
                         32   39       Assessment Account
                         41   44       Assessment Code
                         45   54       Refund Amount
                         55   80       Filler

H. Contractor shall prepare and maintain a complete

file of all input records including the calculated total

annual assessment revenues. All of such information shall be

in the format identified in Exhibit "B" or such format as may

be approved by the County of Fresno and District.

I. Contractor shall provide on June 15 of each

year of this Agreement one test run of the Preliminary Annual

Assessment Roll and on July 15 of each year, one such test

run of the Final Annual Assessment Roll for review and correction by the District prior to transmittal of the Final

Annual Assessment Roll magnetic computer tape to the County

6

of Fresno. Each of such test runs shall be supplied to Dis-
trict as a hard copy printout. At the request of District,
the Contractor shall provide to District additional test runs
as necessary to amend, monitor or analyze total assessment
revenues, revenue by land use or zone of benefit or other
aspects of the assessment program.

     J. The Contractor shall develop and maintain the
Supplemental File as a separate file containing information
necessary for parcel identification or assessment calculation
which is not already contained in or produced by the Master
File, March 1 lien date roll, NAV File or AIS File. Such
information includes but is not limited to, multi-family
residential parcels with more than ninety-nine units and
parcels for which land use or acreage information has been
provided by the District pursuant to Subparagraph 2.D. here-
inabove. Said File shall be updated by the Contractor as
such information becomes available to it and shall be main-
tained by the Contractor to ensure availability of such data
to accurately compute assessments for parcels requiring such
supplemental data. The file shall be delivered to the Dis-
trict in the manner and along with the supporting information
described in Subparagraph A. hereinabove.

     K. Contractor shall be required to submit to Dis-
trict in magnetic tape form and in finished hard copy print-
out form each Final Annual Assessment Roll transmitted to the
County of Fresno in magnetic Computer tape form. Such print-
out shall include all data required by Subparagraph 2C(1)
herein and be submitted along with the supporting information
described in Subparagraph 2.A. hereinabove.




     7

L. A qualified representative of the Contractor
shall attend such meetings and conferences related to the
assessment program as District deems appropriate.

M. By June 1, 1987, or later if authorized in
writing, Contractor shall place the District on-line in the
District office to permit access to the Master File to review
and update property information. Such on-line service shall
be available twelve months per year. On-line property informa-
tion shall include, as a minimum, assessor's parcel number,
zone of benefit, land use, parcel size, owner's name and
address, assessment factor, assessment rate, assessment amount
narrative and situs. Further, on each Tuesday during the
term hereof, Contractor shall deliver to District a written
report of all changes made during the prior week in said
on-line property information. On-line access shall be avail-
able within fifteen (15) seconds for at least ninety percent
(90%) of District's inquiries.

3. ACCEPTANCE TESTING PLAN.

The services to be provided by Contractor pursuant to
Subparagraph 2.A. above ("Preliminary Services") shall be deemed
to have been accepted by the District upon the completion of a
formal acceptance test and written notification from the District
as set forth herein, which formal acceptance test shall be con-
ducted on the following terms: (i) Contractor shall notify the
District in writing that the Preliminary Services are ready for
acceptance testing; (ii) upon receipt of such notice the District
shall test the Preliminary Services in a manner it deems appropri-
ate for a period not to exceed two (2) calendar weeks; (iii) upon
the expiration of such two (2) week period the District shall
either certify to Contractor that the Preliminary Services are
accepted or deliver to Contractor a written description of any

8

specific claimed defects in the Preliminary Services, which defects
shall be limited to failure of the Preliminary Services to produce
an accurate calculation of the Final Annual Assessment Roll; (iv)
upon receipt of such written description Contractor shall deter-
mine whether any of such claimed defects are bona fide defects,
and if so shall proceed immediately to remedy the same (in a period
not to exceed ten (10) working days), whereupon the formal accep-
tance test procedure may again be run. Certification by the Dis-
trict that the Preliminary Services are accepted shall constitute
successful completion of the formal acceptance test.

4. DUTIES OF THE DISTRICT.

A. The District shall make payment to the Contrac-
tor as provided for in this Agreement.

B. The District shall make available to the Con-
tractor any documents, studies or other information in its
possession related to Zones of Benefit and the calculation of
property assessments.

C. The District shall review on a timely basis all
test runs and reports prepared by Contractor.

D. The District shall prepare a map or maps iden-
tifying the boundaries of each Zone of Benefit. The bound-
aries of such zones of benefit shall be digitized or other-
wise identify the zones of benefit which may from time-to-
time be applicable to each parcel to be assessed.

E. The District authorizes Contractor to obtain
during each year of the term hereof, from the County of Fresno
on the District's behalf, a magnetic computer tape file of
the March 1 lien date roll and the related NAV and AIS files
for all parcels within each zone of benefit. Data from the
AIS files shall be limited to that relevant to the calcula-
tion of the subject assessments.

9

F. District shall prepare and submit to Contractor, no later than March 1 of each year during the term of this Agreement, the schedule of assessment factors and rates which shall be used by Contractor in the calculation of property assessments.

G. District shall no later than May 1 of each year during the term of this Agreement supply to Contractor acreage and land use data for those parcels lacking such information in the County NAV or AIS files.

H. District shall provide all modum(s), adapters (if necessary), lines and terminals required to obtain on-line access into Contractor's computer system.

5. TERMINATION OF AGREEMENT WITHOUT CAUSE. The District may terminate this Agreement without cause at any time by giving at least fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the Contractor by certified mail, return receipt requested. In the event of such termination, Contractor shall deliver to the District all finished and unfinished documents, computer software programming and other materials prepared pursuant to this Agreement and not previously delivered to it. Upon such delivery, Contractor shall be compensated pursuant to Exhibit "C" hereto for work satisfactorily performed.

6. TERMINATION OF AGREEMENT FOR CAUSE. In the event Contractor fails to fully perform any of its duties under this Agreement within the time frame specified herein or fails to correct any other breach of its duties hereunder within five (5) days after written notice from District to do so, District may, at its option, deduct from sums due to Contractor those liquidated damages prescribed in Paragraph 12 hereinbelow, and/or (i) withhold payment to Contractor hereunder until Contractor has corrected such breach; (ii) terminate this Agreement forthwith by five (5) days written

10

notice; or (iii) have the work necessary to correct such breach
done by another person, firm or corporation and charge Contractor
therefor. The foregoing remedies are cumulative and not in lieu
of any other remedies which may be available to the District at
law or in equity.

7. NONASSIGNABILITY. Contractor shall not assign this
contract, any obligation hereunder, or any monies which may become
due hereunder, without the prior written consent of District.

8. CONFIDENTIALITY. All reports, files, information,
programs or other data received by the Contractor from the County
of Fresno or the District, or prepared for the District under this
Agreement, are the confidential property of the District and shall
not be made available to any individual or organization by the
Contractor without the prior written approval of the District.
Contractor's obligations hereunder shall survive the termination
of this Agreement.

9. OWNERSHIP, PUBLICATION, REPRODUCTION AND USE OF
INFORMATION AND PROGRAMMING. The District shall own and have the
unrestricted authority to publish, disclose, distribute and other-
wise use, in whole or in part, all materials developed, maintained
or used pursuant to this Agreement, including but not limited to
all flow charts, reports, data, schedules, instructions, manuals
and programming. District shall have the right to mark any work
of Contractor produced hereunder "1987 Fresno Metropolitan Flood
Control District" and to otherwise copyright the same. Contractor
shall not in any way publish, disclose, distribute or otherwise
use such materials.

10. COMPENSATION FOR SERVICES.

A. The Contractor shall bill the District in
accordance with the billing schedule attached hereto as Exhibit "C". A "file item" shall be considered a single

11

assessor's parcel number and all data associated therewith.

All payments shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days

after receipt of Contractor's invoice.

B. Anything to the contrary herein notwithstand-

ing, District shall pay Contractor no more than Ten Thousand

Four Hundred ($10,400.00) in the aggregate for performing the

Preliminary Services. District shall have no obligation to

pay Contractor for any Preliminary Services until such Ser-

vices have been completed to the satisfaction of the District

in accordance with the acceptance testing plan set forth in

paragraph 3 hereinabove.

11. CHANGES. The District may, in its sole discretion,

at any time by written change order make any changes in the scope

of the work required by this Agreement. If any change under this

section causes an increase or decrease in the Contractor's perfor-

mance obligations, District and Contractor shall negotiate to

establish a corresponding adjustment in Contractor's compensation

due hereunder.

12. TIME OF COMPLETION; LIQUIDATED DAMAGES. Time is of

the essence in carrying out the terms of this Agreement. All work

required of the Contractor under the terms of this Agreement shall

be due on or before the dates identified herein in Section 2,

Paragraphs "A" through "M," inclusive. It is agreed that it is

impractical or extremely difficult to ascertain the damages to be

sustained by District in the event Contractor fails to complete

its duties hereunder in the time and manner specified. Therefore,

it is agreed that if Contractor fails to perform its duties listed

in said Paragraph "A" through "M" inclusive within the respective

time frames set forth therein, to the extent that such delay is

not caused by the District, the County or their respective offi-

cers, agents, employees or representatives, Contractor agrees to


12

sections.question:
CONTINUED

2. May I participate in decision making process when F.M.F.C.D.:

A. Is seeking to expand its district size in the unincorporated area of the County?

B. Requires part of Fresno County's property tax base on the land included in the Districts expansion to be transferred to F.M.F.C.D.?

Thank you very much for your prompt attention to this matter.
sections.analysis:
In 1961, the Cities of Fresno and Clovis and the County of Fresno adopted as an element of the Fresno-Clovis Metropolitan Area General Plan, a storm drainage master plan prepared by the Flood Control District for the Metropolitan area. In 1969, these agencies adopted virtually identical drainage fee ordinances to provide for the funding of planned drainage facilities concurrently with development activity which occasioned the need for such facilities.

The local drainage plan shows the drainage area boundaries. Also shown are proposed and existing master plan facilities in each watershed area. An area currently receiving permanent drainage service from existing master plan facilities is crosshatched on the plan.

The rate structure associated with each master plan drainage area is based on the estimated total cost of the urban drainage system including land improvements and engineering. The costs of proposed facilities are estimated and existing facilities are included at actual cost. The total cost is divided into the total land area using appropriate proportionate ratios between varying land uses.

It is important to note that drainage fees paid pursuant to the Fresno Metropolitan Flood Control District administered ordinance are deposited into a separate trust account. The monies are not commingled with District general funds, are not used to fund any administration, operations or maintenance costs, and must be expended within the drainage area in which they are paid.

The fee ordinance, enacted under the authority of the State Subdivision Map Act, requires the identification of the estimated or actual cost of the planned facilities through the adoption of a resolution. Because the fees are based on the cost of facilities, the resolution serves also as the schedule of fees.

The Ordinance permits annual adjustments in the schedule of costs and fees based upon changes in the National Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index or based upon locally experienced changes in system costs. The adjustment is to be made March 1st of each year.

Fresno Metropolitan Flood Control District has recommended that the ENR Index update be used this year. They state that changes in the Master Plan are minimal relative to the need to recalculate system costs for the 130 drainage systems. The adjustment is uniform 2.2% increase pursuant to the index.

OTHER REVIEWING AGENCIES:

The 2.2% increase of ENR Index has been reviewed and approved by the BIA.

FRESNO COUNTY GRAND JURY

ROOM 103, COURTHOUSE
FRESNO, CALIFORNIA 93721

February 16, 1990

The Honorable Mario Olmos
Presiding Judge of the Superior Court
County of Fresno, Department 6
1100 Van Ness
Fresno, California 93721

A. Vernon Conrad, 1989 Chairman
Fresno County Board of Supervisors
2281 Tulare Street
Fresno, California 93721

Dear Judge Olmos and Chairman Conrad:

The 1989-90 Fresno County Grand Jury (Grand Jury) is issuing this Final Report #2, in answer to Supervisor Conrad's request to investigate allegations between Board of Supervisor's Member and the County Administrative Officer. The Grand Jury has focused its investigation on County policy and procedure.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: CONTINUED

2. May I participate in decision making process when F.M.F.C.D.:

A. Is seeking to expand its district size in the unincorporated area of the County?

B. Requires part of Fresno County's property tax base on the land included in the Districts expansion to be transferred to F.M.F.C.D.?

Thank you very much for your prompt attention to this matter.
embedding.first_500_words: **March 15, 1990** **CALIFORNIA F.P.P.C.** Legal Division 428 "J" Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, California 95814 Subject: Expansion of Opinion in File No. I-89-688 In the previous opinion you ruled that I am a subcontractor for a project that involves the Fresno Metropolitan Flood Control District (F.M.F.C.D.). I currently participate in the project as the system analyst. I'm also named as a member of the project team as per the contract (copy previously sent as per option I-89-688). In the last twelve months, the project has been a source of income to me greater than $250.00. In opinion I-89-688 (footnote 6) you advised that because of public agency exemption a conflict does not exist between me as a member of the Board of Supervisors and my role as a subcontractor with F.M.F.C.D. However, I feel the finding needs more examination. **ISSUE** The County Board of Supervisors has discretionary powers over the annual distribution of the special district property tax augmentation funds. F.M.F.C.D. is one of the agencies within Fresno County contending for these funds. In addition when F.M.F.C.D. seeks to add land to its district and receive a percentage from the property tax base, it must receive approval from the Fresno County Board of Supervisors. **QUESTION** 1. May I participate in the decision making process for the distribution of the property tax augmentation funds to: A. F.M.F.C.D. as a recipient of the funds? B. All other districts that are contending for the same funds? QUESTION CONTINUED 2. May I participate in decision making process when F.M.F.C.D.: A. Is seeking to expand its district size in the unincorporated area of the County? B. Requires part of Fresno County's property tax base on the land included in the Districts expansion to be transferred to F.M.F.C.D.? Thank you very much for your prompt attention to this matter. Sincerely, Doug Vagim DOUG VAGIM Fresno County Supervisor :cwr April 6, 1990 Mr. John Wallace, Esq. California F.P.P.C. P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804-807 Re: Letter No. 90-207 Dear Mr. Wallace: I am forwarding to you, as per our conversation, the agenda item that dealt with M.F.C.D. asking for a 2.2% increase in its drainage fees. Please add this question in with the others I previously sent to you on this matter. In addition, for informational purposes, I am including a finding by the Fresno County Grand Jury questioning my participation with the special district augmentation fund and M.F.C.D. Thank you for your help. Sincerely, Doug Vagim **Agenda Item** **County of Fresno** DATE: March 27, 1990 TO: Board of Supervisors FROM: Richard D. Welton, Director Public Works and Development Services Department SUBJECT: Adoption of Resolution Establishing a New Schedule of Drainage Costs and Fees pursuant to Section 17.64.050 of the County Ordinance Code RECOMMENDED ACTION: Adopt a resolution establishing the per-acre costs and fees related to the Storm Drainage and Flood Control Master Plan. FISCAL IMPACT: No fiscal impact to the County. The schedule of costs and fees for the funding of planned drainage facilities is required
